sections.paragraph:
ἐπιστάτης, ου, ὁ (s. prec. entry; Hom. et al., freq. as an administrative t.t.; used for var. officials in lit., ins, pap, LXX; Jos., Ant. 8, 59, C. Ap. 2, 177) in Lk six times in the voc. ἐπιστάτα as a title addressed to Jesus, nearly always by the disciples (the synopt. parallels have διδάσκαλε [cp. Ammonius, 100 a.d., p. 45 Valck. and Philo, Poster. Cai. 54 ἐπ. κ. διδάσκαλοι], κύριε, ῥαββί) master (cp. IG XII/1, 43, 21f ἐπιστάταν τῶν παιδῶν; IPriene 112, 73ff [after 84 b.c.] ἐ. τῶν ἐφήβων whose task was τὰς ψυχὰς πρὸς ἀρετὴν προάγεσθαι; Rouffiac p. 56f.—Diod S 3, 72, 1 Aristaeus the tutor of Dionysus; 3, 73, 4 Olympus the tutor of Zeus; and 10, 3, 4 Pherecydes the teacher of Pythagoras are all called ἐπιστάτης) Lk 5:5; 8:24, 45; 9:33, 49; 17:13.—OGlombitza, ZNW 49, ’58, 275–78.—DELG s.v. ἵστημι. M-M. TW.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

var. var. = various(ly)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

voc. voc. = vocative

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Ammonius Ammonius , phil., V a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

IPriene IPriene = Die Inschriften von Priene—List 3

Rouffiac Rouffiac = JR., Recherches sur les caractères du Grec dans le NT d’après les inscriptions de Priène—List 3

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 381
